items.front: 9-2-15: Who gets special use frequencies? (4 groups)
items.back: 1) USAF/USN/ANG single-pilot jet formations at night/IMC (5+ USAF deploying = any time). 2) U-2/B-57 pressure suit flights at all altitudes (except terminal). 3) All aircraft during supersonic flight. 4) E-3A AWACS MRU per LOA.
Adjacent ARTCCs must NOT have the same special use frequency.

items.front: 9-2-16: Controller duties for hazardous radiation areas (2)
items.back: 1) Advise pilot when flight path traverses radiation area; reroute if requested. 2) Inform pilot when landing airfield is in radiation area; request pilot advise intentions.

items.front: 9-2-17: What does SAMP call sign mean?
items.back: Aerial SAMpling/Surveying for nuclear, chemical, or hazardous material contamination. Provide special handling.

items.front: 9-2-17: How to handle SAMP aircraft clearance requests
items.back: Approve inflight altitude and route change requests to the MAXIMUM extent possible. Other IFR aircraft MAY be recleared to accommodate SAMP requests.

items.front: 9-2-18: How to identify AWACS/NORAD Special flights
items.back: "AWACS/NORAD SPECIAL" as the FIRST item in the remarks section of the flight plan.

items.front: 9-2-18: Acceptable control actions for AWACS/NORAD Special
items.back: Do NOT delay. Altitude changes +/-2,000 ft from requested FL. Radar vectors or minor route changes not impeding progress to destination orbit.
NWSOP = National Winter Storms Operations Plan (winter storms). NHOP = National Hurricane Operations Plan (hurricanes/tropical cyclones).
1) Altitude. 2) Direction of flight. 3) ETA at point closest to drop area (or fix/intersection where drop will occur).

items.front: 9-2-19: Who delays dropsonde release for traffic?
items.back: The AIRCRAFT COMMANDER — based solely upon traffic as issued by ATC.
At/above FL 250, along 60 NM segment. Zigzag both sides of centerline. Lateral ≤12 NM. Altitude ±1,000 ft (2,000 ft block).

items.front: 9-2-20: Basis for approving evasive action maneuver
items.back: ONLY on the basis of a permissible traffic situation. Must specify route segment, max deviation distance, and altitude.
Clear to the BREAKUP FIX as the clearance limit. Forward route/altitude data beyond breakup point as routine flight plan info.
500 feet between each aircraft in the formation.

items.front: 9-2-21: If breakup cannot be approved at breakup fix
items.back: Issue clearance for the flight to continue as a formation.
